question: For the quarterly platform engagement distribution, which statement is best supported by the 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: The chart allows comparison of the proportions of each metric within a quarter but not their absolute numbers across quarters.
options.B: The combined height of each bar shows which quarter had the highest overall platform activity.
options.C: Because each bar reaches 100%, the absolute volume of each metric is the same in all quarters.
options.D: Q1 has the highest number of user registrations compared to the other quarters because its green segment is largest.

explanation: 100% stacking normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. Valid inferences include within-quarter ranking (e.g., one metric > another in every quarter). Invalid inferences include cross-quarter total comparisons or combining unrelated categories.
